[METADATA/INTRO]: Fun fact: this was originally a military 4x4 project but difficult economic situation back in the 90s meant it got canned... and turned into a massive luxury SUV. You could call this the Russian Hummer. BTW, modern versions (with even...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Fun fact: this was originally a military 4x4 project but difficult economic situation back in the 90s meant it got canned... and turned into a massive luxury SUV. You could call this the Russian Hummer. 
 BTW, modern versions (with even more questionable bells and whistles) are (or were?) made by Latvian company Dartz which claims to be the successor of Russo-Balt from the pre-1917 times :) 
 | Car type | SUV | 
 | Curb weight | 3750 kg (8267 lbs) | 
 | Dimensions | 5.15 m (203 in) long, 2.10 m (83 in) wide, 1.95 m (77 in) high |

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2015-06-09
[TITLE]: Peek Inside Celeb's Choice: Luxury Armored Vehicles
[METADATA/INTRO]: Peek Inside Celeb’s Choice: Luxury Armored Vehicles The world’s uber-rich will stop at nothing to protect their loved ones. As a result, a large number of celebrities and those who rank in the affluent one percent are demanding luxury armored...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: In the past, armored vehicles were only utilized by government officials. However, more and more celebrities and VIP clientele are investing in armored automobiles. According to Germany’s Alpha Armouring, 40 percent of its clients are superstars, who demand luxury amenities. As a result of a growing clientele, there are more than 100,000 armored vehicles currently in use worldwide, as stated by International Armoring Corporation. 
 In 2013, Kanye West, one of hip-hop’s highest paid rapper, purchased two Iron Diamonds, a Dartz Motors’ armored vehicle. West reportedly spent $2.4 million on the armored vehicles. The Iron Diamond, which is one of the seven types of Dartz Motors’ Prombron, features bulletproof windows, an auto climate control, an internal information system, full electric options and more. Some models have completely done away with the driver’s door to avoid kidnapping, hijacking and other problems.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2024-04-02
[TITLE]: The Rambo Lambo Returns - Hagerty Media
[METADATA/INTRO]: Media | Articles The Rambo Lambo Returns...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Film fans may recognize Dartz from The Dictator and A Good Day to Die Hard where its armored automobiles featured on the silver screen and the company’s website is no less theatrical, boasting of its 155-year history of royal duty, providing “bulletproof opulence trusted by billionaires, rulers, celebrities, superstars and one Dictator since 1869.” 
 The Iron Diamond’s exterior resemblance to the original Rambo Lambo is uncanny, although the interior is likely to be more practical than the cramped cabin of the LM002—and certainly more luxurious. Dartz is known for using the most ostentatious materials, including snakeskin, ostrich leather, crocodile skin and even whale penis. Not a car for vegans then.
